name: Filleted Trout with Macadamia Nuts and Fried Capers
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Fish stock, OR half clam juice
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large skillet, melt about 3 tablespoons of butter over medium heat.
2. When the butter is nearly sizzling, add the trout fillets and sauté on one side for about 3 minutes.
3. Cover the pan for 1 minute, then turn the fillets and continue sautéing on the other side for another 3-4 minutes total.
4. Season the fish with salt and pepper, then remove to a warm serving plate.
5. Add the sliced Macadamia nuts to the hot skillet and toast them for about 30 seconds.
6. Return the trout fillets to the pan to coat them in the toasted nut mixture.
7. In a separate small, sturdy pan, heat enough oil so that a fine mesh strainer can be lowered into it.
8. Allow the capers to drain in the strainer.
9. Return to the trout skillet and add a splash of white wine.
10. Add the fish stock (or clam juice) and heavy cream; reduce over medium heat until slightly thickened, stirring in the tomato sauce and additional butter.
11. When reduced, spoon the pan sauce over the trout fillets.
12. Quickly lower the fried capers into the hot oil and let them sizzle for a few seconds before removing the strainer.
13. Arrange the fried capers in neat piles at either end of the plate with the trout.
14. Serve immediately.
